title: Kangaroo Level 3-4 - Practice Set 13: Numbers and Geometry
disclaimer: Original practice problems inspired by Math Kangaroo format. Not official AKSF content.

questions.question_text: Nina has 14 marbles. She gives 5 to her brother and then finds 3 more on the floor. How many marbles does Nina have now?
questions.solution: Start with 14, subtract 5 to get 9, then add 3 to get 12.

questions.question_text: How many small squares make up the shape shown?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='50' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='10' y='30' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='30' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='50' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='50' y='30' width='20' height='20'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: An L-shape composed of seven small unit squares.
questions.solution: Count each small square in the figure: there are 7 squares in total.

questions.question_text: Which number should replace the question mark in the sequence 2, 5, 8, 11, ?, 17?
questions.solution: Each term increases by 3, so 11 + 3 = 14.

questions.question_text: A movie starts at 3:15 p.m. and lasts 1 hour and 40 minutes. At what time does the movie end?
questions.solution: Add 1 hour to 3:15 to get 4:15, then add 40 minutes to get 4:55 p.m.

questions.question_text: A notebook costs 2 dollars and a pen costs 1 dollar. Mia buys 3 notebooks and 2 pens. How much does she pay?
questions.solution: 3 notebooks cost 3 x 2 = 6 dollars. 2 pens cost 2 x 1 = 2 dollars. Total is 6 + 2 = 8 dollars.

questions.question_text: How many triangles are in the picture?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 120 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><polygon points='60,10 10,70 110,70'/><line x1='60' y1='10' x2='60' y2='70'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A large triangle divided by a vertical line from the top vertex to the base.
questions.solution: There are 2 small triangles plus the 1 large outer triangle, for a total of 3.

questions.question_text: Tom is taller than Ana. Ana is taller than Leo. Who is the shortest?
questions.solution: Tom > Ana > Leo, so Leo is the shortest.

questions.question_text: A pencil is 15 cm long. A crayon is 6 cm shorter than the pencil. How long is the crayon?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 60' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='15' width='120' height='10'/><rect x='10' y='35' width='80' height='10'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: Two horizontal bars: a longer one representing the pencil and a shorter one representing the crayon.

questions.question_text: The sum of three different one-digit numbers is 20. The largest of the three numbers is 9. What is the smallest?
questions.solution: The other two sum to 11. Since they must be different one-digit numbers both less than 9, possible pairs sum to 11: (4,7) or (5,6). To get the smallest value among the three numbers we maximize flexibility; the minimum value 4 appears in the pair (4,7), so the smallest is 4.

questions.question_text: A rectangle has a perimeter of 24 cm. Its width is 4 cm. What is its length?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 160 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='20' y='20' width='120' height='40'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A horizontal rectangle taller in width than in height.
questions.solution: Perimeter = 2(length + width), so 24 = 2(L + 4), giving L + 4 = 12, so L = 8 cm.

questions.question_text: A pizza is cut into 8 equal slices. Sam eats 3 slices and Eva eats 2 slices. What fraction of the pizza is left?
questions.image_description: A circle divided into 8 equal slices by four diameters.
questions.solution: They ate 3 + 2 = 5 slices, so 8 - 5 = 3 slices are left. That is 3/8 of the pizza.

questions.question_text: Four children each have one of these pets: cat, dog, bird, fish. Ben does not have the cat or the dog. Ben does not have the fish. What pet does Ben have?
questions.solution: Ben does not have the cat, dog, or fish. The only remaining pet is the bird.

questions.question_text: In the shape pattern square, circle, triangle, square, circle, triangle, ..., what is the 20th shape?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 40' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><circle cx='50' cy='20' r='10'/><polygon points='80,30 90,10 100,30'/><rect x='110' y='10' width='20' height='20'/><circle cx='150' cy='20' r='10'/><polygon points='180,30 190,10 200,30'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: The repeating pattern: square, circle, triangle, repeated twice.
questions.solution: The pattern repeats every 3 shapes. 20 divided by 3 gives a remainder of 2, so the 20th is the 2nd in the cycle, which is the circle.

questions.question_text: Leo has coins of 10 cents and 25 cents. He has 5 coins that total 80 cents. How many 25-cent coins does he have?
questions.solution: Let x be the number of 25-cent coins. Then 25x + 10(5 - x) = 80, so 15x + 50 = 80, giving 15x = 30, so x = 2.

questions.question_text: Jim poured 250 mL of water into a jug. Then he added 400 mL more. How many milliliters of water are in the jug?
How many small cubes are used to build this staircase?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 140 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='70' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='70' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='50' y='70' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='30' y='50' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='50' y='50' width='20' height='20'/><rect x='50' y='30' width='20' height='20'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A staircase shape built from unit cubes forming a 3-2-1 pyramid from the side.
questions.solution: The staircase has 3 cubes on the bottom, 2 in the middle, and 1 on top: 3 + 2 + 1 = 6 cubes.

questions.question_text: The product of two whole numbers is 24, and their sum is 11. What is the larger number?
questions.solution: Factor pairs of 24: (1,24), (2,12), (3,8), (4,6). The pair with sum 11 is (3, 8). The larger number is 8.

questions.question_text: A square piece of paper is folded in half, then in half again. How many small rectangles appear when it is unfolded?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 80 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='60' height='60'/><line x1='40' y1='10' x2='40' y2='70'/><line x1='10' y1='40' x2='70' y2='40'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A square divided into 4 equal smaller squares by one horizontal and one vertical line.
questions.solution: Folding in half twice creates two perpendicular creases, dividing the square into 4 equal small rectangles (squares are rectangles).

questions.question_text: Five friends shook hands with each other exactly once. How many handshakes happened in total?
questions.solution: Each of 5 people shakes hands with 4 others, giving 5 x 4 = 20, but each handshake is counted twice, so 20 / 2 = 10 handshakes.

questions.question_text: Lina writes the numbers 1, 2, 3, ..., 30 in order. How many times does she write the digit 2?
questions.solution: Digit 2 in units place: 2, 12, 22 (3 times). Digit 2 in tens place: 20-29 (10 times). Total 3 + 10 = 13.

questions.question_text: A rope is 2 meters long. It is cut into pieces of 25 cm each. How many pieces are there?
questions.image_description: A long rope marked with equally spaced cut lines.
questions.solution: 2 meters equals 200 cm. 200 divided by 25 equals 8 pieces.

questions.question_text: A jar has 20 candies. Mia eats half of them. Then her brother eats half of what is left. How many candies remain?
questions.solution: Half of 20 is 10, so 10 remain. Half of 10 is 5, so 5 remain.

questions.question_text: A clock shows 10:45. What time will it show in 2 hours and 30 minutes?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 100 100' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><circle cx='50' cy='50' r='40'/><line x1='50' y1='50' x2='50' y2='20'/><line x1='50' y1='50' x2='75' y2='60'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A round analog clock face with hour and minute hands.
questions.solution: 10:45 plus 2 hours is 12:45. Then 12:45 plus 30 minutes is 1:15.

questions.question_text: A square has a side of 6 cm. It is divided into 4 equal smaller squares. What is the perimeter of one small square?
questions.image_svg: <svg viewBox='0 0 80 80' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='none' stroke='currentColor' stroke-width='2'><rect x='10' y='10' width='60' height='60'/><line x1='40' y1='10' x2='40' y2='70'/><line x1='10' y1='40' x2='70' y2='40'/></g></svg>
questions.image_description: A square divided into four equal smaller squares.
questions.solution: Each small square has side 6 / 2 = 3 cm. Perimeter is 4 x 3 = 12 cm.
